reason: Critic-approved leader, ready for submission packaging.
title: Ship leader bundle and open probe-head architecture axis
hypothesis: The prior fusion axis is exhausted. The immediate priority is packaging the critic-approved leader (run-0003-0001, val ROC-AUC 0.6652) for submission. After securing a submission slot, the next gains are likely from probe-head architecture improvements (MLP regularization without prior fusion, based on the coverage-MLP context card showing 0.672 on a different split) and fixing the abnormally high training temperature (7.39) identified in the conditional lead.

problem_frame.title: Probe-head architecture and revised coverage strategy
problem_frame.root_cause: Calibrated prior fusion v2 (run-0002-0002) achieved val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.6648, a -0.0005 regression versus the current leader run-0003-0001 (0.6652). The prior fusion component itself is near-random on train (prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc=0.487) and only marginally above raw probe on validation (val_prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc=0.662), confirming that the Bayesian prior calibration has not extracted meaningful signal. The prior fusion post-processing lane should be deprioritized in favor of shipping the existing raw-probe leader bundle and pursuing class-coverage or probe-head improvements.
problem_frame.hypothesis: The prior fusion post-processing lane has been tested twice with consistent negative results. Both negative vetoes at confidence 0.68 confirm this axis should be closed. The immediate priority is shipping the critic-approved raw-probe leader bundle (run-0003-0001 at 0.6652) to secure a submission slot. After that, plan should target high-value axes: probe-head architecture improvements (MLP regularization without prior fusion) and revised class-coverage strategies, since brute-force 52→75 expansion was flat. Collapsing to another calibration tweak would waste experiment capacity with no signal basis.
adopt_now: [card:research-run-0003-0002-prior-fusion-weight-calibration-md-adopt-now] Ship run-0003-0001 (leader at 0.6652) or run-0002-0001 raw-probe submission bundle. Use raw probe output exclusively — prior fusion degrades it (0.6622). The bundle is already validated and critic-approved.

[card:01-validated-findings-md-perch-and-embedding-findings] Embedding-head style models can add complementary signal even when their standalone score is not the best (require @ 0.88). Keep the perch embedding probe as the primary signal source.
[memory:memory-0002] Primary data_coverage branch is the leader at 0.665216 with critic=approved. This is the submission candidate to bundle first.
consider: [card:03-next-experiment-priors-md-perch-probe-lane] If fitted classes lag active classes, expand coverage before micro-tuning. Coverage expansion from 52→75 classes was flat, so further coverage gains may require different class selection strategy rather than brute-force expansion.

[card:research-run-0002-0003-coverage-mlp-combined-full-75-class-mlp-probe-md-current-state] The combined coverage expansion + MLP regularization branch achieved 0.672 on a different metric context — worth investigating whether MLP regularization alone (without prior fusion) could lift the probe head.
[memory:memory-0001] Baseline run validated at 0.6651 with submission_status=candidate_created. This is a fallback submission if the leader bundle has packaging issues.
reject: [card:research-run-0003-0002-prior-fusion-weight-calibration-md-root-cause] Prior fusion weight calibration regresses by -0.003 vs leader. Do not allocate further submission slots or experiment iterations to prior fusion calibration until a fundamentally different prior source or calibration method is available.

The prior fusion component (val_prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc=0.662) is below the raw probe baseline, confirming it adds negative value as currently implemented. Any future prior fusion work must first demonstrate standalone lift on validation before being blended.
Padded_cmap remains very low (0.062) across all runs — segment-level localization is not the bottleneck for ROC-AUC but should not be a focus area until the primary metric plateau is broken.

candidate_branches.reason: Critic-approved leader, ready for submission packaging.

candidate_branches.title: Submission bundle: run-0003-0001 leader
candidate_branches.hypothesis: Package the critic-approved leader run-0003-0001 (val ROC-AUC 0.6652) as a submission bundle using raw probe output exclusively. No prior fusion blending.
candidate_branches.reason: Two adopt-now cards at 0.68 confidence and branch memory confirm the leader is ready for submission. This secures a leaderboard slot before experimenting on new axes.
MLP regularization without prior fusion — isolates signal from coverage+MLP context card.
Abnormally high temperature (7.39) may suppress discriminative signal. Diagnostic ablation.

candidate_branches.title: MLP probe-head regularization without prior fusion
candidate_branches.hypothesis: Replacing the linear probe head with a regularized MLP (2-layer, dropout=0.3, weight_decay=1e-4) without any prior fusion may lift val ROC-AUC above 0.6652. The coverage+MLP context card showed 0.672 on a different split, suggesting the MLP head has independent value.
candidate_branches.reason: The context card for run-0002-0003 (coverage+MLP combined) suggests MLP regularization has independent signal. Isolating it from prior fusion removes the vetoed component while potentially retaining the gains.

candidate_branches.title: Temperature audit: reduce from 7.39 to 1.5
candidate_branches.hypothesis: The training temperature of 7.39 is abnormally high and likely inflates logits toward near-uniform distributions, suppressing discriminative signal. Reducing to 1.5 on the run-0002 code state may recover meaningful probe-head signal and lift val ROC-AUC.
candidate_branches.reason: The conditional lead from run-0006-0005 identifies temperature as a potential root cause for inflated logits. This is a low-risk, high-diagnostic-value ablation that could explain the gap between train (0.992) and val (0.665) ROC-AUC.
